name: Electric Ladyland: The Museum of Fluorescent Art
description: The world's only museum devoted to the wonders of fluorescence.

name: Nieuwe Spiegelstraat
description: An Amsterdam street lined with antique shops each one dedicated to a different obscure collection.

name: De Poezenboot (The Cat Boat)
description: A floating cat sanctuary on an Amsterdam canal.

name: Hortus Botanicus Amsterdam
description: Established in 1638 to battle the Black Death, this botanical garden is a treasure trove of rare flora.

name: The Torture Museum
description: Museum preserving torture methods from the past.

name: KattenKabinet
description: Cat art as far as the eye can see.

name: Cuypers Library
description: This gorgeous reading room houses the oldest and largest art history library in the Netherlands.

name: Micropia
description: The world's first zoo for microbes collecting the horrible and wonderful things living on and around us.

name: Oudemanhuispoort
description: An ancient passage filled with stalls selling used books, once frequented by Vincent Van Gogh.

name: Museum Vrolik
description: A curious collection dedicated to human and animal anatomy and pathology.

name: In't Aepjen
description: This historic Amsterdam bar used to accept sailor's monkeys as payment.

name: Café Papeneiland
description: Famed for its pie, this eatery has a hidden tunnel that allowed Catholics to secretly walk to church during the Reformation.

name: A'DAM Lookout and Over The Edge Swing
description: This 22-floor tower's Skydeck offers panoramic views of Amsterdam.

name: Amsterdam Oersoep
description: A Dutch passageway's artistic, aquatic-themed makeover pays tribute to the capital city's famed waterways.

name: Our Lord in the Attic
description: The attic of a four-century-old canal house hides a clandestine church.

name: Amsterdam Cheese Museum
description: This cheese shop also houses a museum devoted to Dutch dairy.

name: The Hash, Marijuana and Hemp Museum
description: The history and future of cannabis.

name: Beth Haim Cemetery
description: A Portuguese Jewish cemetery filled with stunning grave monuments that date back to 1614.

name: Theater Tuschinski
description: A beautifully eclectic mix of architectural styles.

name: Brouwerij ’t IJ
description: This bird-loving brewery is situated in an old municipal bathhouse, nestled beside a 300-year-old windmill.

name: Red Light Secrets
description: This museum housed in a former brothel explores the past and present of sex work in Amsterdam.

name: Bibliotheca Philosophica Hermetica (Ritman Library)
description: This impressive collection of rare books on ancient mysticism inspired author Dan Brown.

name: Museum Tot Zover
description: This museum inside Amsterdam's largest cemetery explores how people deal with death.

name: Paternoster Lift
description: This unusual elevator doesn’t stop or slow down.

name: The Little Woodcutter
description: This unattributed sculpture in Amsterdam is hiding in plain sight.

name: Pizzeria San Marco
description: Sail in for a ’dam good slice.

name: Giethoorn
description: This small town in the Netherlands has no roads but instead, miles of canals and over 100 bridges.

name: Van Gogh Bicycle Path
description: Inspired by "The Starry Night" this Dutch bike path turns into a glowing work of art each night.

name: Mark Bischof's Studio
description: This subterranean studio is filled with fascinating kinetic art.

name: Kleine Trippenhuis
description: One of Amsterdam's narrowest houses is located right across the canal from its widest.

name: The Resistance Museum
description: A poignant museum dedicated to the history of the Dutch resistance against Nazi occupation

name: REM Eiland
description: This pirate-broadcasting-tower-turned-restaurant played a rebellious role in the history of Dutch television.

name: Book Mountain
description: Peek through a transparent pyramid and drink in the mountain made of books.

name: Oudekerksplein
description: This city square is home to Amsterdam's oldest church and a statue honoring Dutch sex workers.

name: Homomonument
description: Three connected pink triangles memorialize persecuted gays and lesbians throughout history.

name: Huis te Vraag Cemetery
description: An overgrown and largely forgotten cemetery lies hidden within the outskirts of Amsterdam.

name: Labyrint Drielandenpunt
description: A beautiful maze marks where three countries meet.

name: Amsterdam Tulip Museum
description: Tucked in the back of a flower shop, its collection details the fascinating history of the Netherlands's iconic symbol.

name: City Hall Urinal
description: A public urinal so pretty, it was declared a national monument.

name: Keith Haring Mural
description: This massive mural by famed pop-artist Keith Haring was hidden for almost 30 years until its 2018 rediscovery.

name: Bloemen Route
description: The Bloemen Route, or “Flower Route”, stretches 25 miles through prime Dutch tulip country as a result of “tulip mania,” the world’s first economic bubble.

name: Biblioteca Ets Haim
description: The world's oldest Jewish library was founded by Sephardic Jews in 17th century Amsterdam.

name: Hotel Inntel Zaandam
description: Colorful and chaotic stacked hotel in North Holland.

name: Pianola Museum
description: A unique collection of gilded age mechanical pianos.

name: De Waag
description: Amsterdam's former hotspot for public executions and dissections.

name: Celestial Vault
description: Hidden within a Netherlands crater is a stone bed that lets those who lie in it see the sky as a curved surface.

name: Zevenlandenhuizen (Seven Country Houses)
description: A set of seven houses, each built in a style that represents a European country.

name: Teylers Museum
description: Oldest Museum of the Netherlands is a true cabinet of curiosities.

name: Cube Houses
description: These slanted cubic abodes are a creative if odd solution to a zoning pickle.

name: Keukenhof Gardens
description: Unsurprisingly, Holland is home to one of the most magnificent flower gardens in the world.

name: Selexyz Dominicanen
description: This centuries-old gothic monster has been converted into a sprawling, glossy bookstore.

name: Six Collection
description: A rare tour of an aristocrat's private home reveals valuable 17th-century paintings from artists like Rembrandt.

name: Normaal Amsterdams Peil (Amsterdam Ordnance Datum)
description: Europe's official "zero-level," the equator of elevation.

name: Windmills of Kinderdijk
description: Birthplace of the "Cat in the Cradle" and a cluster of iconic, original windmills that let the Dutch tame the seas.

name: Singel 7
description: Amsterdam's narrowest house facade is a monument to 17th-century tax evasion.

name: Museum Speelklok
description: This Netherlands museum holds a gloriously mechanical collection of ornate musical automata.

name: Cuyperspassage
description: Some 80,000 Delft Blue tiles line the inside of this Amsterdam walkway.

name: The House with The Blood Stains
description: The former home of a mad diplomat is said to still be marked in bloody, arcane scrawls.

name: Museum Boerhaave
description: A former plague hospital turned medical museum.

name: Amsterdam Gable Stones
description: In the days before house numbers, these colorful signs held clues about various buildings' purposes.

name: Leiden's Floating Christmas Market
description: A market built atop boats is one of the few floating holiday markets in Europe.

name: Mozesbrug (Moses Bridge)
description: Invisible bridge parts the waters, invoking biblical images and preserving the ominous feel of an ancient moat.

name: Fort Bourtange
description: This beautifully designed star-shaped fort was the premier defense system of the late 1500s.

name: Trompenburg Arboretum
description: A true Valhalla for cactus and succulent lovers.

name: Oost-Indisch Huis
description: The former headquarters of history's oldest megacorporation.

name: Kasteelruine Fluweelengrot
description: The ruins of this Dutch castle sit atop a labyrinth of once-secret tunnels and passageways, dating back over 900 years.

name: Jheronimus Bosch Art Center
description: Take a stroll through the hellish grotesques of Hieronymus Bosch in this elaborate Netherlands church.

name: Hortus Botanicus Leiden
description: A 'living museum' in Leiden, the oldest of its kind in the Netherlands.

name: Corpus Museum
description: The world’s first interactive human body museum also serves as a chair for a giant orange man.

name: Bijbels Museum
description: A nomadic museum known for its art exhibitions about religion and faith.

name: Silly Walks Tunnel
description: Crouch down, stretch tall, and be sure to flail your legs to pay homage to the Monty Python classic.

name: The Wadden Sea
description: Rich with fauna and flora, nature-lovers can walk across the sea at this UNESCO World Heritage spot.

name: Witches' Weigh House
description: The giant scales in this museum can still tell if someone is one of the devil's brides just by weight.

name: Begijnhof Chapel
description: A hidden church in a hidden courtyard tells the story of the Miracle of Amsterdam.

name: Doloris's Meta Maze
description: A dream-like maze full of strange art, surreal spaces, and fantasy landscapes to get lost in.

name: Amsterdam Ordnance Datum
description: This large copper bolt represents the benchmark for measuring sea levels in most of Europe.

name: Kremlin of the Netherlands
description: This miniature take on the Kremlin is one Dutch man's retirement project.

name: St. Pietersberg Caves
description: Incredible labyrinth of man made caves.

name: Sonnenborgh Observatory
description: A historic observatory now an astronomy museum beautifully situated at the moat of the Middle Age city centre.

name: Schiphol Clock
description: It appears as if a man is standing behind this giant airport clock, painting the hands in real time.

name: Madurodam
description: A miniature city, once ruled by the Queen of the Netherlands.

name: Museum Romeinse Katakomben (Roman Catacombs Museum)
description: Visit the ancient, mysterious Roman catacombs ... in the Netherlands.

name: Efteling Sprookjesbos
description: This fairy tale forest hides scenes from some of the more obscure and bizarre Western nursery stories.

name: Koninklijke Wachtkamer Amsterdam Centraal
description: Where royalty waits in the height of luxury.

name: Huddesteen
description: The last of Amsterdam's original sea level markers remaining in place.

name: Pyramid of Austerlitz
description: A grassy pyramid named after a French battle.

name: Betondorp
description: This 1920s Amsterdam neighborhood was built as a giant experiment in concrete construction.

name: Apenheul Primate Park
description: Go inside a revolutionary, cage-less zoo where 35 species live and some of them roam free alongside their human visitors.

name: Pijpencabinet (Amsterdam Pipe Museum)
description: This vast collection of smoking pipes from across the world spans centuries.

name: Hans Brinker Budget Hotel
description: Debatably clever, or just plain honest hotel advertised as "The Worst Hotel in the World."

name: 'Beetle Sphere'
description: A unique work of automotive art calls this shopping center home.

name: Brederode Castle
description: The ruins of a medieval castle that once belonged to one of the most powerful families in the Netherlands.

name: Museum de Gevangenpoort (The Prison Gate Museum)
description: A seven-century-old jail tells a not-so-pleasant history of crime and punishment.

name: Strawberry Drive-In
description: Drive past the giant fruit sculptures and up to the window for a menu of fruity treats.

name: Castle Rosendael
description: A late-medieval Dutch castle with trick fountains in its whimsical water gardens.

name: Evoluon
description: A UFO-shaped event building originally intended to be a science museum showcasing the future.

name: Het Rariteiten Kabinet
description: Strange secondhand shop showcasing taxidermy and oddies

name: Waterfall of Loenen
description: The tallest waterfall in the Netherlands is a lovely, stepped feature that is only 50 feet tall.

name: Bolwoningen
description: This neighborhood of futuristic ball houses looks like an alien settlement in a science fiction film.

name: Martin Luther King Park Statue
description: This bronze statue of Martin Luther King was placed in the park without permission.

name: Van Kleef Museum and Distillery
description: Rumored to have been frequented by Vincent van Gogh, it's the only remaining genever producer in the Hague.

name: The Hidden Eschers of Leiden
description: Tucked away in the Leiden city hall are a group of works by famed artist MC Escher.

name: Wrakkenmuseum
description: Pull up and have a drink at a museum filled with hundreds of years' worth of shipwreck artifacts.

name: Capsule Hotel
description: Survival capsules find a second life as a unique hotel.

name: Floating Farm
description: The world's first floating farm is staffed by one human, three robots, and 35 cows.

name: Castle d'Oultremont
description: This medieval castle was painted pink as part of a now-abandoned children's theme park.

name: Fort Saint Pieter
description: This historical Netherlands fortress still bears the marks of its single siege.

name: The Linked Headstones of Het Oude Kerkhof
description: Two gravestones clasp hands over the religious divide.

name: The Giant Of Rotterdam
description: A statue of the second-tallest Dutchman in history.

name: Wolfsdreuvik
description: A monument hidden in the woods built in 1938 as a getaway for a Dutch museum curator.

name: Tovertunnel (Magic Tunnel)
description: This colorfully-painted tunnel links two distinct parts of a historic neighborhood.

name: Vlissingen Wind Organ
description: A set of 27 bamboo tubes creates an eerie soundscape that changes with the wind.

name: Librije Chained Library
description: Unchanged for centuries, this medieval library keeps its books in chains.

name: Kruisherenhotel
description: A luxury hotel housed in a former 15th century Gothic church.

name: Leiden Observatory
description: Devious political maneuvering and four ancient, working telescopes are central to the world's second-oldest observatory.

name: Preserved Mummies of the Hervormde Kerk
description: A bizarre form of natural mummification was discovered in the church of this Frisian village.

name: Café De Dokter
description: The smallest bar in Amsterdam has stayed in the family since 1798.

name: Plompe Toren
description: Its town washed away centuries ago, this lonely tower by the sea tells the tale of a mermaid and a curse.

name: National Holocaust Monument
description: The mirrored section takes the shape of Hebrew characters when viewed from above.

name: Lange Duinen
description: This large sand dune in the Netherlands is home to wind-whipped trees with "floating" roots.

name: De Riekermolen
description: A beautifully rebuilt windmill from the 17th century, situated along the Amstel River.

name: Amsterdam Toy Museum
description: This collection of 20th-century toys is filled with a lifelong personal toy collection brought from the U.S. to Amsterdam.

name: Apparent Dead House in the Hague
description: A preserved building where the dead were once watched for signs of life.

name: Eisinga Planetarium
description: Home to the world's oldest functioning orrery.

name: The BMW
description: A dramatic art installation features a BMW flying out of a building in Rotterdam.

name: Gouwe Aquaduct
description: Yup, that's a 330-foot yacht passing over the highway.

name: Royal FloraHolland
description: The largest flower auction house in the world.

name: Tramlijn 8
description: A line number in Amsterdam that will likely never be used again due to its tragic history.

name: Radio Kootwijk
description: This disused VLF radio station is no longer in service but the surrounding town still bears its name.

name: Het Badkonijn
description: A giant floating rubber duck-bunny painted with a different design every year.

name: 'Zhdun' (Homunculus Loxodontus)
description: This Dutch art piece became a Russian icon.

name: Excalibur Climbing Wall
description: This imposing tower named after the mythical sword of King Arthur is considered the tallest freestanding climbing wall in the world.

name: Crane Hotel
description: Live out your childhood construction fantasies in this one-of-a-kind gyrating hotel.

name: Poem With No End
description: 'The Letters of Utrecht' lays out a ribbon of poetry one letter at a time, with no final verse in sight.

name: Mission Museum Steyl
description: This massive collection of taxidermy is full of specimens collected by missionaries during their international travels.

name: The Clog Museum
description: A Dutch clog company in the town of Zaandam loves the wooden shoes so much it made a museum for them.

name: Grote Kerk
description: This towering church is home to a world-famous organ and a treasure trove of medieval art.

name: Panorama Mesdag
description: The largest 19th century panorama in the world still in-situ.

name: Rietveld-Schröder House
description: This still-celebrated home was a radical architectural experiment in 3D De Stijl.

name: Miniature Walcheren
description: A 1:20 scale piece of the Netherlands commissioned by a queen lets visitors take a stroll as giants.

name: De Wieden National Park Observation Tower
description: Repurposed space and ultra-modern construction collide to create this geometric wooden spectacle.

name: Uniastate Bears
description: This steel frame is a skeletal mirage of a lost 16th-century stone building.

name: Temple of Taffeh
description: An ancient Egyptian temple sits within a modern European museum.

name: The UFO of Inktpot
description: Atop this former dutch railway office sits an interstellar work of art.

name: STRAAT
description: The world’s largest street art museum.

name: Yde Girl
description: Remarkably preserved body discovered in a peat bog.

name: The Luchtsingel
description: Built by crowdsourcing, this wooden foot bridge saved a part of Rotterdam that was languishing in wait of city funding.

name: Veenmuseum
description: Hop aboard a charming bog train to explore this rare open-air museum of peat.

name: Spijkenisser Eurobruggen (Euro Banknote Bridges)
description: The bridges portrayed on EU currency aren't fictional anymore.

name: Planetarium Zuylenburg
description: This functional solar system model was inspired by the famous Eisinga Planetarium.

name: 'De Bospoldervos'
description: A massive stone fox oversees a Rotterdam elementary school crosswalk.

name: Green Cathedral
description: Lombardy poplars planted to imitate the size and shape of the Cathedral of Notre-Dame de Reims.

name: The Delftse Poort
description: A modern reimagining of a medieval city gate.

name: Hotel de Vrouwe van Stavoren
description: A Netherlands hotel where you can sleep inside a wine barrel.

name: Paul McCarthy's 'Santa Claus'
description: Rotterdammers' dirty minds gave this Santa a dirty nickname and cursed it to a life of wandering.

name: Leiden’s Wall Formulae
description: Science meets wall art to highlight the beauty of famous mathematical equations.

name: Fikkie
description: This charming pup is meant to celebrate the youth and stubbornness of the city's students.

name: Lapin Botanique
description: The place where houses are born.

name: Rotterdam Historic Harbor
description: Old ships and machinery fill a historic harbor in the city that's home to Europe's largest port.

name: Paradiso
description: Rejoice at Amsterdam's ‘pop temple,’ a former church reborn as a raucous music venue.

name: Louwman Museum
description: In the Netherlands, one of the world's largest private car collections can be found.

name: The Polaroid
description: An enormous Polaroid picture adds an artistic flair to Rotterdam.

name: Schoonoord Historical Garden
description: An early 19th-century English style garden hidden in the Rotterdam cityscape.

name: Bizarium
description: This museum is filled with an eclectic collection of unique inventions.

name: Maeslantkering
description: This enormous storm barrier is one of the largest moving structures on Earth, with each arm taller than the Washington Monument.

name: Ecokathedraal
description: Nestled in the woods lies this land art project: a hybrid between park, maze and natural monument.

name: Waterloopbos
description: This former hydraulic laboratory is now a woodland dotted with old engineering models.

name: Hotel New York
description: This hotel was once home to a steamliner company and became the final rest stop for Europeans heading to the New World.

name: The Wall Poems of Leiden
description: The works of poetry's biggest names grace the walls of the city of Leiden, all carefully painted by hand.

name: Planes@Plaza
description: This old KLM airplane cut into three parts was transformed into an eclectic airport gift shop.

name: Cigar Band House
description: Mosaics created from 11 million cigar bands.

name: Sylvette
description: A nearly 25-foot concrete sculpture of Sylvette, Picasso's muse.

name: West Kruiskade Teapot
description: A huge teapot illuminates a home for the elderly.

name: Shipwreck and Beachcombing Museum
description: The history of sea-faring refuse and disaster is on cramped display in this Netherlands folk museum.

name: Het Knopenlaantje
description: Where lovers tie birch branches into long-lived knots.

name: The Kilsdonk Mill
description: A rare double mill nestled along the banks of the Aa river.

name: Buitenschot Land Art Park
description: An ingenious Dutch land art project uses grass ridges to cut airport noise pollution in half.

name: Slauerhoffbrug Flying Drawbridge
description: This unique bridge physically lifts a piece of the road into the air to let boats pass by.

name: Groene Bedstee
description: A set of beech hedges planted in the 19th century forms a tunnel that takes on a different appearance in every season.

name: European Space Research and Technology Centre
description: The birthplace of most all of Europe's space missions is here on the Dutch coast.

name: Schaakstukkenmuseum (The Chessmen Museum)
description: This small museum located under the famous cube houses, is home to some 30,000 chess pieces.

name: Plein Rond de Eik
description: The gargoyles and grotesques of the 21st century are here—22 emoji built into a new building in the Netherlands.

name: Miffy Traffic Light
description: The cutest way to stay safe in traffic.

name: World Peace Flame Pathway
description: Lumps of significant stone from around the world surround a flaming monument for peace.

name: Afsluitdijk
description: The 20-mile causeway that separates the Netherlands from the sea.

name: Huis Doorn
description: The elegant manor house where Kaiser Wilhelm II lived in exile after World War I.

name: Bibliotheca Thysiana
description: This public library and its impressive collection of academic books have not changed since the 17th century.

name: Sint-Willibrordkerk
description: A uniquely preserved neo-Gothic church that is now home to a collection of art and woodcarvings.

name: World's Largest Mosaic Egg
description: Made of 41,900 pieces of mosaic, it took two years to piece together this giant egg.

name: Café De Vergulde Kruik (The Gilded Jar Café)
description: One of the Leiden's oldest pubs is also the birthplace of the Heineken star.

name: Mr. Kaor's Portrait
description: The portrait of a man from Japan who sent weekly letters to a Dutch hotel he never visited.

name: Lab 111
description: An independent cinema housed in a former pathological anatomy laboratory.

name: Rotterdam's Old City Wall
description: Inside this metro station is a section of the city's former medieval fortification.

name: Gebouw Leidsch Dagblad Elephant Drainpipe
description: This historic Dutch newspaper building is covered with media-themed details—and one unrelated oddity.

name: Bottle-ship Museum
description: The world's largest collection of ships in bottles.

name: Kraatpaal
description: A strangely curved lamppost stands tall over this avenue in Rotterdam.

name: Victoria Amazonica
description: People have been placing babies on these lilies for photoshoots since the Victorian era.

name: Stadsbank van Lening (City Bank of Lending)
description: The facade of this bank has changed little over the centuries, including its unique welcome sign.

name: Black Tulip Museum
description: A journey through 430 years of bulbiculture in the Netherlands.

name: New Ocean Paradise
description: A condensed piece of China floating in a Dutch harbor.

name: The Van Gogh Church
description: Stained-glass windows inspired by Vincent van Gogh's paintings decorate this church in the town where the artist's career began.

name: DOMunder Subterranean Ruins
description: Underground attraction offering tours of the foundational ruins of a church nave that collapsed in 1674.

name: Whale Jaw of Schiermonnikoog
description: An awe-inspiring giant blue whale jaw bone stands in the center of this Dutch island.

name: Burcht van Leiden
description: This 11th-century castle sits so low you can't see it behind the surrounding buildings.

name: Louwman Historic Telescopes
description: The world's largest private telescope collection hides in a secret wing of a Dutch auto museum.

name: Schouwburgplein Kinetic Lamp Posts
description: Four giant movable street lights decorate this modern Rotterdam square.

name: Sexy Loo
description: These public toilets also provide entertainment for visitors to Amsterdam’s Red Light District.

name: Tij Bird Observatory
description: Watch the local Netherlands birds from inside this giant eco-egg.

name: Masonic Temple Loge La Vertu
description: One of the oldest masonic temples in the Netherlands, built in 1757.

name: Basiliek Oudenbosch
description: Scale model of St. Peter's Cathedral (Rome) in the middle of a small Dutch town.

name: Eusebius Church
description: Organisms normally seen under a microscope decorate this church.

name: Einstein's Sink
description: A lecture hall sink once used by Einstein and other noted physicists is now regarded as a strange science relic.

name: Schepenzaal
description: This stately council chamber hasn't changed since 1545.

name: Aletta Henrïette Jacobs
description: This plaque marks the former home of one of the first women physicians in the Netherlands.

name: Kabouterhuisje van Elmer (Elmer's Gnome House)
description: A tiny house showcases the busy life of a local gnome.

name: Achterhoeks Planetarium
description: An impressive array of unique homemade planetariums.

name: Groningen University Museum
description: Preserving the research tools, unexpected inventions, and odd collections of generations of college professors.

name: Blaak House
description: Elegant marble and old vaults fill this historic Dutch bank turned office space.

name: Czar Peter House
description: The place where Czar Peter I of Russia resided in 1697 during his Grand Embassy.

name: Exposure
description: This giant wire frame man sculpture is crouched in contemplation.

name: Leiden Stones
description: A trio of red, white, and blue stones embedded in the streets of Leiden are markers of medieval territory and law.

name: Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope
description: A radio telescope for listening to the stars, based in one of the cloudiest countries in the world.

name: Mauritstoren
description: The location of the world's first telescope demonstration.

name: J. Boom
description: A museum-like shop that has been producing clogs, brushes, and ropes for nearly 200 years.

name: 'Saved by a Whale’s Tail'
description: This unique sculpture proved invaluable when it saved a train from careening off the tracks.

name: Trompettersteeg
description: Widely advertised as Amsterdam’s narrowest street, this passage was once a highlight of the Red Light District before its windows closed.

name: Museum Prinsenhof Delft
description: Now a museum, this building was once a 14th-century monastery and the place where the founder of the Dutch nation lived and was assassinated.

name: Central Station Bicycle Parking
description: The world's largest bicycle shelter can hold more than 12,500 two-wheelers.

name: LOFAR Superterp
description: The nerve center of a massive, state-of-the art radio telescope that sprawls across Western Europe.

name: Maastunnel
description: The Netherlands' first car tunnel has an unusually rectangular shape.

name: 3D-Printed Bike Bridge
description: The Netherlands is home to a bridge printed out of reinforced concrete.

name: Schokland
description: The struggle of a small Dutch island against the sea.

name: Biopik
description: A giant yellow phallus sculpture hidden in the gardens of the Museum Boijmans Van Beuningen.

name: Natuurbrug Zanderij Crailoo
description: The world's longest wildlife overpass provides Hilversum's critters with a safe alternative to trying their luck on the roadway.

name: Bataviawerf
description: Building Men of War and Indiamen from Holland's Golden Age.

name: Zweetkamertje (Sweat Room)
description: Graffiti covers the walls of a room where university students through the centuries have waited nervously to defend their theses.

name: Belgian Monument
description: This gigantic monument hidden in the woods was built to thank the Dutch for sheltering Belgian refugees during World War I.

name: Rozenburg Wind Wall
description: The Rozenberg wind wall in South Holland makes for smooth sailing through the Calandbrug canal.

name: Chet Baker Memorial Relief
description: In 1988, jazz legend Chet Baker fell to his death from a window of this hotel facing Amsterdam Centraal.

name: Graveyard Groenesteeg
description: This early 19th-century graveyard is the final resting place of Vincent van Gogh's mother.

name: The DAF Museum
description: Walk through the history of a Dutch truck manufacturer, from 1928 to today.

name: De Brandgrens (The Blaze Boundary)
description: These lights mark the area of Rotterdam that was bombed and burned in 1940.

name: Rotterdam Jazz Artists Memorial
description: Portraits of musicians line Oude Binnenweg, the jazziest street in the city.

name: Kunsthalte (Witte de Withstraat Tram Stop)
description: An art installation memorializing a tram stop, which became a tram stop.

name: World's Largest Christmas Bauble
description: Every winter, this former planetarium is transformed into a massive, brightly-lit Christmas ornament.

name: Vaalserberg
description: The tallest mountain in mainland Netherlands isn't actually very tall.

name: Greccio Museum of Devotional Items
description: This small museum collects the Christian remembrances which would otherwise be forgotten.

name: Oudt Leyden
description: This institution has been serving up some of the best pancakes in the Netherlands for over a century.

name: Rijnlandse Roede (Rhenish Rod)
description: An unassuming rod in the Netherlands was once the old standard unit of length for the entire Dutch empire.

name: Vorstengraf
description: One of the largest burial mounds in the Netherlands and Belgium.

name: IJsseloog
description: This artificial ring island was built to hold cubic tons of polluted dirt.

name: 'Bagage'
description: A striking memorial to the Jews of Leiden who were murdered during World War II is scattered throughout the city.

name: Sweepers Clock
description: The hands on this clock appear to be created by two workers sweeping debris into careful positions.

name: Laagste Punt van Nederland (Lowest Point in the Netherlands)
description: A large measuring stick marks the lowest point of a country that is partially under sea level.

name: Hooglandse Kerk Clock
description: Once housed in the towers of this Gothic church, this 17th century clock still ticks today.

name: Tree Trunk Garden House
description: Dutch woodpile home in the forest.

name: Noletmolen
description: Wind power station for making famous Ketel One vodka.

name: Hotel de Bilderberg
description: An idyllic hotel that gave its name to a supposed shadow government.

name: Gerbrandy Tower
description: For the holidays, the tallest broadcasting tower in the Netherlands turns into one of the world's tallest Christmas trees.

name: Diamond Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the history of the diamond trade in the Netherlands.

name: Van der Wall Telescope
description: Once the world's biggest telescope, it was later used as a coat hanger by a famous Dutch astronomer.

name: Nederlands Openluchtmuseum
description: This large open-air museum showcases the ordinary lives of the people of the Netherlands and pre-industrial crafts and practices.

name: Leiden's Monumental Urinal
description: This public toilet built in the famous Amsterdam School architectural style is a Dutch national monument.

name: HomeComputerMuseum
description: An interactive museum dedicated to preserving and sharing the history of the home computer.

name: Archeological Park Matilo
description: It's forbidden to dig within this ancient Roman fort hidden beneath a playground.

name: Van Nelle Factory
description: An icon of International Style architecture on the outskirts of Rotterdam.

name: De Hef
description: A bridge that has seen many reiterations over its lifetime.

name: The Old Leiden Belltower
description: The remains of a medieval wall discovered in the basement of a jewelry store.

name: World Soil Museum
description: A Dutch museum preserves the world's most diverse collection of vertical slices of soil.

name: Pinetum Blijdenstein
description: Most complete collection of Gymnosperms in the world.

name: Einstein's Chair
description: Albert Einstein would often sit in this chair during visits to the observatory to discuss science with Willem de Sitter.

name: Reactor Institute Delft
description: A nuclear reactor created for Eisenhower's "Atoms for Peace" campaign is now used for research at a Dutch university.

name: Dwingeloo Radio Observatory
description: This giant radio telescope would be a rusting ruin if it hadn't become such a beloved relic.

name: Aqueduct Veluwemeer
description: While most bridges allow roads to pass over water, this one allows water to pass over a road.

name: Ehrenfest's Signature Wall
description: For over 100 years the most important physicists in the world have autographed this university wall.

name: Defensiekanaal
description: This vast World War II defense line was the site of a fierce battle.

name: The Hat of Lou Bandy
description: From afar it looks like someone's hat blew into the water, but closer inspection reveals a giant floating sculpture.

name: Belfort Sluis
description: This bell tower is the only one of its kind in the Netherlands and was a symbol of prestige.

name: Willebrord Snellius' Grave
description: One of the great math minds of the 17th century is underfoot in this 900 year old Dutch church.

name: It Lân Skip
description: A farm turned upside down.

name: Museum Depot
description: Unknown to many, this is where items not displayed in a Dutch history of science museum are held.

name: Ehrenfesthuis (Ehrenfest House)
description: The house of physicists Paul Ehrenfest was also a temporary dwelling and study for his good friend Albert Einstein.

name: Draaiorgel Museum
description: At this barrel organ museum, history and live concert keep Dutch street organ culture alive.

name: National Videogame Museum
description: A multi-story, retro videogame time capsule in the Netherlands.

name: Vermeer's Grave
description: The grave of the famous painter is marked by a simple stone in the ground.

name: Het Wittehuis
description: The oldest skyscraper of Europe now stands dwarfed by the surrounding structures.

name: Hidden Basement in Smit
description: A retail shop has preserved the 17th-century food cellar from the old building it now occupies.

name: Gravensteen's Whipping Pole
description: A relic of the old jail still stands in its original location.

name: Anatomisch Museum Leiden
description: Some of the specimens on display at the Leiden University Medical Center have been helping educate physicians since the 17th century.

name: Castellum Fectio
description: For years, the remains of this ancient Roman fortress were hidden under an orchard.

name: Kröller-Müller Museum
description: Located inside Hoge Veluwe National Park, this museum began as the private collection of a prominent female art collector.

name: Houseboat Museum Amsterdam
description: Discover what life is like on Amsterdam’s famous canals.

name: Herkules Windmill
description: This Dutch windmill has its roots in an American invention.

name: Rijn en Lek Windmill
description: This may be the world's only drive-through windmill.

name: Leiden Gunpowder Disaster Memorial
description: A massive explosion that leveled hundreds of buildings in the city of Leiden is remembered by a waterside plaque.

name: Lady of Stavoren
description: This Dutch statue is a monument to the fictional shrew who brought doom to the city of Stavoren.

name: Transvaal Boer
description: Above the heads of passersby stands a small statue commemorating soldiers from the Boer War.

name: Verfmolen De Kat
description: This windmill has been grinding pigments for traditional paints for more than 200 years.

name: Internationaal Klompenmuseum (International Clog Museum)
description: The largest collection of the iconic wooden shoe in the world.

name: Dutch Pinball Museum
description: This museum features over 100 playable pinball machines, some dating back to the 1850s.

name: Het Roze Huisje (Pink House)
description: This 17th-century house has a long and colorful history.

name: Zandverhalen Sandsculpture Museum
description: One of the largest sandsculpture installations in the world, this exhibit boasts a unique combination of sprawling sand scenes, theological musings, and salvaged shipwrecks.

name: FireFly
description: A solid steel plane designed to catch fire over and over again.

name: De Leidse Zeemijn (Leiden's Naval Mine)
description: The remnants of a WWI naval mine that once terrorized the country now decorates a street.

name: Grote Kerk Cannonballs
description: The Siege of Naarden ended in 1814, but the cannonballs embedded in the town church remain.

name: Gateway Diner Beatrixpark
description: This diner from 1930s America now resides in a small town in the Netherlands.

name: Onze Lieve Vrouwetoren (The Tower of Our Lady)
description: At the base of this tower, a plaque marks the original center point of the Netherlands.

name: Erasmus Statue
description: The oldest statue in the Netherlands has survived relocation, bombardment, and burial.

name: Tim Cantor Gallery
description: A wonderland of oil paintings by a self-taught artist who collaborates with Imagine Dragons—hidden in a 17th-century building near the Rembrandt House.

name: Fish Doorbell at Weerdsluis Lock
description: The world's first fish doorbell monitors this water passageway.

name: Sandsculptures Garderen
description: Various sculptures are a rotating exhibit in this small town.

name: Ludolph van Ceulen Memorial
description: A 14th-century church holds a unique monument to a German mathematician who dedicated his life to calculating the value of pi.

name: De stal van Stier Herman
description: The world's first transgenic bovine has been preserved at the museum where he spent his final years—though his old stable is now a cafe.

name: Radio Bloemendaal
description: Small transmitter operated by a priest in the Netherlands.

name: Mirakelkolom
description: A pillar is all that remains of a church built to honor a peculiar miracle.

name: Middellandse Zeetuin Uilenbroek
description: A family-run botanical garden with tropical plants, artsy sculptures, and eccentric buildings.

name: Valkhofbunker
description: This World War II-era bunker hides between medieval chapels in Nijmegen's city center.

name: Velorama National Bicycle Museum
description: The only bicycle museum in the Netherlands charts the invention of the now-ubiquitous two-wheeled machine.

name: Vrouwekerk
description: Only a few walls remain of the last church the Pilgrims used before they sailed to North America.

name: De Kwal (The Jellyfish)
description: This jellyfish provides clean water for an entire Dutch province.

name: Kamerlingh Onnes Laboratory Plaque
description: A plaque hidden in the law faculty at Leiden University honors the first scientist to make liquid helium.

name: McCammonplein
description: A unique World War II memorial commemorates where the American B-17 bomber Man-O-War crashed.

name: Dixie Flyer Crash
description: Found in the waddenzee, this propeller has been put on public display as a reminder of the Second World War.

name: Kunstwerk 't Draaiend Huys
description: What looks like an ordinary house from the outside is actually a rotating sculpture.

name: Street Posts Museum
description: An unexpected public exhibit showcases the diversity of Dutch street posts.

name: Melkfabriek de Hoop
description: This building once stood at the center of a butter war.

name: Kardoes the Hellhound
description: A bronze statue commemorating the legend of a hellhound haunting the village of De Lutte.

name: Beeldenroute Willem Arntszkade
description: How one woman gave orphaned sculptures a new home in the city of Utrecht.

name: The Railway Museum (Spoorwegmuseum)
description: Visitors can still take a train to this former train station turned museum.

name: De Ballonzuil
description: In the middle of a forest you can find the first landing place of an air balloon in the Netherlands.

name: Westergasfabriek
description: One of Amsterdam’s most popular cultural and recreational complexes is on the site of a former gas works.

name: Berend Botje Statue
description: This monument pays homage to a classic Dutch tune.

name: Papiermolen De Schoolmeester
description: The only remaining wind-powered paper mill in the world.

name: Free Tiny Gallery
description: A tiny art gallery where artists can donate their work for the public to take and enjoy.

name: Quarantainestation Heijplaat (Heijplaat Quarantine Center)
description: A 20th-century naval quarantine center is now a local park.

name: 'Flying Pins'
description: These giant yellow bowling pins seem to be defying gravity.

name: ‘Celestial Teapot’
description: A gigantic teapot sitting on top of a parking garage in the center of Utrecht.

name: Museum Het Schip
description: An Amsterdam School landmark combining a museum and social housing.

name: John Frost Bridge
description: The largest airborne operation in history was launched to capture this bridge.

name: Westerveld Crematorium
description: The first crematorium in the Netherlands.

name: Wolvenplein Prison
description: The imposing structure was originally established in 1580 as a military stronghold.

name: Castellum Hoge Woerd
description: This recreated Roman outpost stands above its buried predecessor.

name: Universel Murad Hassil
description: A seemingly out-of-place temple built on the spot where a mystic had a profound spiritual experience.

name: Imitation Airfield
description: This fake airfield was built as a decoy to protect a real airport from the Allied Forces.

name: Hovenring
description: A first-of-its-kind circular bridge for cyclists.

name: De Berenkuil
description: A bike traffic circle showcasing the works of local graffiti artists.

name: Israël Kiek Memorial
description: A memorial commemorating the man whose name became synonymous with taking a picture in the Netherlands.

name: Monument to the Unknown Child
description: Cycling around the Veluwe visitors are treated to a collection of stones from around the world.

name: Church Ruin Oude Niedorp
description: An old, partly destroyed church, preserved in the state it was left in after a fire.

name: Oorlogsmuseum Overloon (Overloon War Museum)
description: Oldest World War 2-themed museum in Western Europe.

name: IJssellinie Fortress
description: This site was constructed as a Cold War-era defense against a Russian invasion.

name: Beer Advertising Museum
description: Enjoy a pint surrounded by Europe’s largest collection of vintage beer advertisements.

name: Drenkelingenhuisje (‘Drowning House’)
description: A storm shelter built for survivors of shipwrecks.

name: Major Bosshardt Monument
description: A statue commemorating 'the greatest inhabitant of Amsterdam of all time.'

name: TextielMuseum
description: Exploring the Netherlands’ textile past and future under one roof.

name: Woerden Elagabal Inscription
description: This ancient stele gives a testimony to the presence of the Syrian sun god Elagabalus on the Roman Empire's western frontier.

name: Moskee van Naarden
description: This former cigarette factory was built in a style inspired by Islamic architecture.

name: Pilgrim Fathers Church
description: This 15th century church is where the Pilgrims spent their last night in the Netherlands before setting sail for America.

name: Luchtwachttoren 5K3
description: A Cold War watchtower atop a WWII-era bunker on a historic Dutch dike.

name: Black Madonna Chapel at Kappelberg
description: In 1421, a drowning man prayed for his life and promised to build a chapel where he would wash ashore.

name: De Hoge Nol Bunker
description: A buried bunker offers both a scenic view and a melancholy reminder of Duindorp’s wartime past.

name: De Tijdtrap
description: A set of parking garage escalators doubling as a historical overview of Rotterdam.

name: Sieboldhuis
description: The west's first Japanese museum and the origin of East Asian studies.

name: Frederikspark
description: The heart of Dutch football history.

name: Schoenenkwartier
description: A place where shoes are not displayed as fashion, but dissected as objects of craft, industry and cultural identity.

name: Proosje van Schiedam
description: A statue of Schiedam's beloved gin mascot.

name: Zendstation Smilde Replica
description: For a moment, you could think there are two identical communication towers in a row, but one of them is made out of PVC.

name: Sovjet Ereveld (Soviet Field of Honor)
description: A cemetery in the Netherlands honors Soviet soldiers from World War II.

name: Verloren Toren
description: Once a forgotten city-wall tower, now the centerpiece of an underground bicycle parking.

name: Vluchtoord
description: The remnants of a refugee settlement for Belgians during WWI.

name: Piet Hein Statue
description: A memorial to the Netherlands' most famous privateer.

name: Tiny Hidden Houses on Westerstraat
description: Seven mini rowhouses fill the gap between 54 and 70 Westerstraat.

name: Penis Fountain
description: Amsterdam's Red Light District leaves little to the imagination.

name: The Totalitarian Art Gallery
description: This basement museum explores the art and artistry of fascism.

name: Tassen Museum of Bags and Purses
description: The largest collection of handbags in the world, the oldest of which were used to carry Bibles, primarily by men.

name: Stichting Nationaal Brilmuseum
description: The spectacular history of spectacles on display.

name: Johan's Ark
description: “And the Lord said unto Johan, build for thee an ark for the end of the world is approaching.”

name: Skatehal Arnhem
description: A long-closed church turned into a saintly skate park.

name: Netherlands Electricity and Radio Museum
description: This small electronics museum is full of whirring, humming, clicking ephemera.

name: Bakkerssteeg Street Sign
description: This century-old street sign is a forgotten remnant of the old city.

name: European Walk of Fame
description: The European equivalent of the Hollywood Walk of Fame is slowly spreading over the entire city.

name: Fruitcorso
description: This annual harvest parade features intricate floats covered with fruits, vegetables, and seeds.
